texte: Following the Franco-Prussian War of 1870-1871, the French army undertook a profound modernisation of its communication resources. The law of 13 March 1875 created the first company of sapper-telegraphers, attached to the 2nd Engineer Regiment in Montpellier. This specialised unit operated the first wired military telegraph networks, marking the birth of the French Transmissions.

titre: Mont-Valérien becomes the centre of the Transmissions
texte: As military telecommunications developed, the companies of sapper-telegraphers were gradually brought together at Fort Mont-Valérien. This site became the main centre for training, experimentation and the development of field networks. For several decades, it formed the technical heart of French military telecommunications.

titre: Wireless Telegraphy and the Eiffel Tower
texte: Captain Gustave Ferrié led the first military experiments in Wireless Telegraphy (TSF) from the top of the Eiffel Tower. The results demonstrated the immense strategic potential of long-distance radio communications. These trials helped save the Eiffel Tower from its planned demolition and opened a new era for military communications.

The French army created a permanent Wireless Telegraphy service, officially establishing the use of radio in military communications. This innovation gradually freed the army from the constraints of wired networks and paved the way for the major developments that would accompany the First World War.
